title: Pipelines DAG, Z.AI Provider & Stability Fixes

highlights.title: Node-based DAG Pipelines
highlights.description: Content pipelines redesigned as a node-based DAG, unlocking richer branching and reuse; includes a fix for semantic search fallback when no vector store is configured.

highlights.title: Z.AI Provider Support
highlights.description: New Z.AI LLM provider with native API endpoint and Bearer auth, auto-registered from environment variables alongside the existing provider adapters.

highlights.title: Dynamic HuggingFace Image Models
highlights.description: Image generation now discovers HuggingFace models dynamically via the huggingface_hub SDK and uses the new router.huggingface.co endpoint, replacing the deprecated api-inference host.

highlights.title: Pipeline & Filter UX Polishing
highlights.description: LLM sections are disabled when no provider is configured, trending terms are filtered with TF-IDF to drop low-signal words, and channel filters now gate on stats to avoid dropping legitimate content.

highlights.title: Per-channel Post Browsing
highlights.description: Channel list gains a quick button to browse all collected posts for a specific channel, making moderation and auditing faster.

highlights.title: Telegram Client Stability
highlights.description: Grace period added to ClientPool.disconnect_all eliminates noisy 'Task was destroyed but it is pending' warnings during shutdown.

highlights.title: Scheduler Module Rename
highlights.description: src/scheduler/manager.py renamed to service.py with a compatibility shim, aligning with the service-layer naming used elsewhere in the project.

highlights.title: Test Infrastructure Overhaul
highlights.description: Deduplicated fixtures, centralized CLI DB patching, and expanded coverage across services, parsers, CLI, and web routes to close long-standing test-infra debt.
